t: Assistance permitted based on necessity
ar: ١٣٩. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): تنزل المعونه علي قدر الميونه.
en: 139. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Assistance is allowed according to need.

t: Moderation prevents destitution
ar: ١٤٠. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): ما عال من اقتصد.
en: 140. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: He who is moderate does not become destitute.

t: Small families ensure ease
ar: ١٤١. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): قله العيال احد اليسارين.
en: 141. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: A small family is one of the ways of (securing) ease.

t: Grief as half of old age
ar: ١٤٣. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): الهم نصف الهرم.
en: 143. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Grief is half of old age.

t: Endurance proportional to affliction
ar: ١٤٤. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): ينزل الصبر علي قدر المصيبه, ومن ضرب يده علي فخذه عند مصيبته حبط اجره.
en: 144. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Endurance comes according to the affliction. He who beats his hand on the thigh in his affliction ruins all his good actions.

t: Mischief mongers' irreproachability
ar: ١٥. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): ما كل مفتون يعاتب.
en: 15. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Every mischief monger cannot even be reproved.

t: Safeguarding belief through charity and prayer
ar: ١٤٦. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): سوسواايمانكم بالصدقه, وحصنوا اموالكم بالزكاه, وادفعوا امواج البلاء بالدعاء.
en: 146. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Protect your belief by charity; guard your wealth by paying Allāh’s share; and ward off the waves of calamity by praying.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: ١٤٧. ومن كلام له (عليه السلام) لكميل بن زياد النخعي
en: 147. Amīr al-mu’minīn’s Conversation with Kumayl Ibn Ziyād an-Nakha‘ī

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: قال كميل بن زياد: اخذ بيدي امير المومنين علي بن ابي طالب (عليه السلام), فاخرجني الي الجبان فلما اصحر تنفس الصعداء ثم قال:
en: Kumayl ibn Ziyād has related: Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, caught hold of my hand and took me to the graveyard. When he had passed through the graveyard and left the city behind, he breathed a deep sigh and said:

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: يا كميل بن زياد, ان هذه القلوب اوعيه فخيرها اوعاها فاحفظ عني ما اقول لك:
en: O Kumayl, these hearts are containers. The best of them is that which preserves (its contents). So, preserve what I say to you:

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: الناس ثلاثه: فعالم رباني ومتعلم علي سبيل نجاه, وهمج رعاع اتباع كل ناعق يميلون مع كل ريح, لم يستضييوا بنور العلم, ولم يلجووا الي ركن وثيق.
en: People are of three types: One is the scholar and divine; then the seeker of knowledge who is also on the way to deliverance. Then (lastly) the common rot who run after every caller and bend in the direction of every wind. They seek no light from the effulgence of knowledge and do not take protection of any reliable support.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: يا كميل, العلم خير من المال: العلم يحرسك وانت تحرس المال, والمال تنقصه النفقه, والعلم يزكوعلي الانفاق, وصنيع المال يزول بزواله.
en: O Kumayl, knowledge is better than wealth. Knowledge guards you, while you have to guard the wealth. Wealth decreases by spending, while knowledge multiplies by spending, and the results of wealth die as wealth decays.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: يا كميل بن زياد معرفه العلم دين يدان به به يكسب الانسان الطاعه في حياته وجميل الاحدوثه بعد وفاته و العلم حاكم والمال محكوم عليه.
en: O Kumayl, knowledge is belief which is acted upon. With it man acquires obedience during his life and a good name after his death. Knowledge is the ruler while wealth is ruled upon.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: يا كميل هلك خزان الاموال وهم احياء و العلماء باقون ما بقي الدهر اعيانهم مفقوده وامثالهم في القلوب موجوده ها ان هاهنا لعلما جما واشار بيده الي صدره لو اصبت له حمله بلي اصبت لقنا غير مامون عليه مستعملا اله الدين للدنيا ومستظهرا بنعم الله علي عباده وبحججه علي اوليايه او منقادا لحمله الحق لا بصيره له في احنايه ينقدح الشك في قلبه لاول عارض من شبهه.
en: O Kumayl, those who amass wealth are dead even though they may be living while those endowed with knowledge will remain as long as the world lives. Their bodies are not available but their figures exist in the hearts. Look, here is a heap of knowledge (and Amīr al-mu’minīn pointed to his bosom). I wish I could get someone to bear it. Yes, I did find (such a one); but either he was one who could not be relied upon. He would exploit the religion for worldly gains, and by virtue of Allāh’s favours on him he would domineer over the people and through Allāh’s pleas he would lord over His devotees. Or he was one who was obedient to the hearers of truth but there was no intelligence in his bosom. At the first appearance of doubt he would entertain misgivings in his heart.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: الا لا ذا ولا ذاك او منهوما باللذه سلس القياد للشهوه او مغرما بالجمع والادخار ليسا من رعاه الدين في شي‏ء اقرب شي‏ء شبها بهما الانعام السايمه كذلك يموت العلم بموت حامليه.
en: So, neither this nor that was good enough. Either the man is eager for pleasures, easily led away by passions, or is covetous for collecting and hoarding wealth. Neither of them has any regard for religion in any matter. The nearest example of these is the loose cattle. This is the way that knowledge dies away with the death of its bearers.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: اللهم بلي! لا تخلو الارض من قايم لله بحجه, اما ظاهرا مشهورا, واما خايفا مغمورا ليلا تبطل حجج الله وبيناته. وكم ذا واين اوليك? اوليك والله الاقلون عددا, والاعظمون عند الله قدرا, يحفظ الله بهم حججه وبيناته, حتي يودعوها نظراءهم, ويزرعوها في قلوب اشباههم,
en: O My Allāh! Yes; but the earth is never devoid of those who maintain Allāh’s plea either openly and reputedly or, being afraid, as hidden in order that Allāh’s pleas and proofs should not be rebutted. How many are they and where are they? By Allāh, they are few in number, but they are great in esteem before Allāh. Through them Allāh guards His pleas and proofs till they entrust them to others like themselves and sow the seeds thereof in the hearts of those who are similar to them.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: هجم بهم العلم علي حقيقه البصيره, وباشروا روح اليقين, واستلانواما استوعره المترفون وانسوا بما استوحش منه الجاهلون, وصحبوا الدنيا بابدان ارواحها معلقه بالمحل الاعلي, اوليك خلفاء الله في ارضه, والدعاه الي دينه,
en: Knowledge has led them to real understanding and so they have associated themselves with the spirit of conviction. They take easy what the easygoing regard as hard. They endear what the ignorant take as strange. They live in this world with their bodies here but their spirits resting in the high above. They are the vicegerents of Allah on His earth and callers to His religion.

t: Amīr al-mu’minīn's wisdom on knowledge and virtue
ar: اه اه شوقا الي رويتهم! انصرف (يا كميل) اذا شيت.
en: Oh, oh, how I yearn to see them! Go away now, O Kumayl, wherever you wish!

t: Self-awareness as the foundation of success
ar: ١٤٩. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): هلك امرو لم يعرف قدره.
en: 149. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: He who does not know his own worth is ruined.

t: Human mortality and inevitable fate
ar: ١٥١. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): لكل امري عاقبه حلوه او مره.
en: 151. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Every human being has to meet the end, sweet or bitter.

t: The transient nature of human existence
ar: ١٥٢. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): لكل مقبل ادبار, وما ادبر كان لم يكن.
en: 152. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Every comer has to return and after returning it is as though he never existed.

t: Endurance ensures eventual success
ar: ١٥٣. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): لا يعدم الصبور الظفر وان طال به الزمان.
en: 153. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The endurer does not miss success although it may take a long time.

t: Adhering to contracts with steadfast fulfillment
ar: ١٥٥. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): اعتصموا بالذمم في اوتادها.
en: 155. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Adhere to contracts and entrust their fulfilment to steadfast persons.

t: Obligation of obedience to the undeniable authority
ar: ١٥٦. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): عليكم بطاعه من لا تعذرون بجهالته.
en: 156. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: On you lies (the obligation of) obedience to the person about whom you cannot plead the excuse of ignorance.

t: Encouraging virtue through kindness and benevolence
ar: ١٥٨. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): عاتب اخاك بالاحسان اليه, واردد شره بالانعام عليه.
en: 158. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Admonish your brother (comrade) by good behaviour towards him, and ward off his evil by favouring him.

t: Authority often leads to partiality
ar: ١٦٠. وقال (عليه ‏السلام): من ملك استاثر.
en: 160. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever obtains authority (usually) adopts partiality.
